restaurant_openings.name: Chicago Wins One Medal During the 2026 James Beard Awards
restaurant_openings.summary: Chef Jacob Potashnick of the highly ambitious Ukrainian Village restaurant Feld brought home Chicago’s lone James Beard Award for 2026. Potashnick earned his medal in the Best Chef: Great Lakes category, where he faced off against a fleet of top talents, including fellow Chicago chef Norman Fenton of Michelin-recognized Latin restaurant Cariño, Vinnie Cimino of […]

restaurant_openings.name: Chicago’s Most Anticipated Restaurant Openings of Summer 2026
restaurant_openings.summary: While Chicago summers are typically reserved for festivals and anywhere with access to the great outdoors, there’s a slate of new restaurant openings on the horizon that are equally worthy of attention. Bonus: Some even have patios of their own. As with some recent additions to Chicago’s hospitality scene, these newcomers don’t limit themselves to […]

restaurant_openings.name: 11 Chicago Restaurant and Bar Openings to Know in June
restaurant_openings.summary: Chicago’s restaurant openings roundup is your resource to find out what’s on its way in . The list is by no means comprehensive. Have information on another opening? Send all tips to chicago@eater.com. LINCOLN PARK— On the edge of Oz Park sits Paper Tiger, a new North Side cafe serving coffee, tea, and frozen matcha […]

restaurant_openings.name: A New Chicago Bakery Channels France With an Edge
restaurant_openings.summary: Walking into West Town’s Guillotine Bakery it’s hard to know where to look first. There’s the sparkly copper U-shaped counter near the front door. Above it hangs six industrial lights emitting a soft yellow glow. An old link chain is delicately draped on one of the earth-toned, color-washed walls. But come Wednesday, June 17, when […]

restaurant_openings.name: An Expansive New Waterfront Restaurant Settles in Along the Chicago River
restaurant_openings.summary: For years, Prime & Provisions owners Luke Stoioff and David Rekhson would look out from their downtown steakhouse towards the north bank of the Chicago River and ask themselves, ‘What if?’ Across multi-lane Wabash Avenue and over the river sat a space with spectacular views — a basement cafeteria that catered to the workers in […]

events_calendar.name: After Devastating Fire, Owner Of Jay’s Backyard BBQ In Austin Looks To Rebuild
events_calendar.summary: A kitchen fire on Friday destroyed much of Jay Young’s 7-year-old restaurant as well as neighboring businesses. A fundraiser has been launched to help Young with repairs.

events_calendar.name: Rated Q remains an unquestionable success
events_calendar.summary: On a bitter winter night in 2023, I settled in at the Music Box Theatre with my best friend to enjoy my first ever Rated Q screening: Twilight (2008). It’s a night I cherish deeply and recount often as one of my most unique movie outings, and since then, I’ve regularly brought new people to […] The post Rated Q remains an unquestionable success appeared first on Chicago Reader .
